questions.q: Which British author wrote the detective novel 'The Murder of Roger Ackroyd,' featuring the famous Belgian detective Hercule Poirot?
What is the name of the magical school that Harry Potter attends in the book series?
choices: Ilvermorny School of Witchcraft and Wizardry
Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry

questions.q: In Jane Austen's 'Pride and Prejudice,' what is the first name of the sharp-witted heroine?

Which artist cut off part of his own ear during a mental health crisis?
In 'The Odyssey' by Homer, what is the name of the island kingdom where Odysseus is king?

What is the name of the narrator in F. Scott Fitzgerald's 'The Great Gatsby'?
In Jane Austen's 'Pride and Prejudice', what is the first name of Mr. Darcy?

Which American author wrote the satirical novel 'Catch-22'?

A mystery involving a missing person

questions.q: Which female author wrote 'Frankenstein', one of the earliest examples of science fiction?
In which year did the Pulitzer Prize for Literature first begin awarding books?
Which Spanish painter's work 'Las Meninas' is considered one of the most important paintings in Western art history?
What is the name of the Gothic novel by Bram Stoker that features the vampire Count Dracula?
Which American author wrote the classic novel 'The Great Gatsby,' featuring the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby?
In which century did the Renaissance art movement primarily flourish in Europe?
Which Pre-Raphaelite painter created 'Ophelia,' depicting the drowning scene from Hamlet with meticulous botanical detail?
In Dante Alighieri's 'Divine Comedy,' what is the name of the poet who guides Dante through Hell and Purgatory?
Which Russian formalist literary theorist wrote the seminal work 'Art as Device' in 1917?
What is the primary subject matter of Caravaggio's 'The Calling of Saint Matthew,' depicting divine intervention in a tavern?
choices: A performance of a biblical play
A conversion through divine light in a mundane setting

questions.q: In Nabokov's 'Pale Fire,' the novel is structured as which poetic form with accompanying commentary?
choices: An epic poem with textual apparatus
A narrative poem with scholarly annotations
A haiku collection with biographical notes
A sonnet sequence with critical essays

questions.q: Which Japanese woodblock artist created the series 'Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji'?
What is the central narrative framing device in Geoffrey Chaucer's 'Canterbury Tales'?
choices: A tournament of knights in Camelot
A merchant's voyage to the Orient
A royal court gathering in Westminster

questions.q: In postmodern literary theory, which French philosopher introduced the concept of 'deconstruction'?
Which abstract expressionist painter is known for his drip painting technique, exemplified in works like 'Autumn Rhythm'?
In Thomas Mann's 'The Magic Mountain,' at what elevation does the sanatorium setting take place?
choices: In the Swiss Alps near Davos

questions.q: Which Metaphysical poet wrote 'The Flea,' a poem using an insect as an extended metaphor for seduction?
What Renaissance artistic principle, meaning 'rebirth of antiquity,' characterized the movement's return to classical sources?
In Toni Morrison's 'Beloved,' what is the historical event that serves as the novel's foundation?
choices: The infanticide of an enslaved child to prevent capture
The Middle Passage across the Atlantic
The Underground Railroad journey to Canada

questions.q: Which Italian Futurist manifesto, published in 1909, celebrated violence, speed, and the rejection of the past?
In postcolonial literary theory, which scholar wrote 'Orientalism,' examining Western representation of the East?
Which modernist poet wrote 'The Waste Land,' employing fragmented structure and mythological allusions?
In Gustave Flaubert's 'Madame Bovary,' what is Emma's primary source of false romantic ideals?
choices: The novels and sentimental literature she consumes

questions.q: Which Byzantine artistic tradition, developed in the 6th century, is exemplified by the mosaics in the Hagia Sophia?
In Samuel Beckett's 'Waiting for Godot,' what is the primary action of the play?
choices: Two characters engage in a philosophical debate
Two characters wait for someone who never arrives
A protagonist undertakes a physical journey
A couple reconciles after years of separation

questions.q: In James Joyce's 'Ulysses', which literary technique of representing the continuous flow of a character's thoughts is prominently employed throughout the novel?
Which Russian author's masterwork 'Crime and Punishment' features the protagonist Raskolnikov committing a premeditated murder?
Which modernist painter's work 'Les Demoiselles d'Avignon' shocked the art world by fragmenting the female form and is often cited as a precursor to Cubism?
In Geoffrey Chaucer's 'The Canterbury Tales', which pilgrim is a knight of high moral standing who has fought in the Crusades?
In William Blake's illuminated work, which prophetic book features the character Los, a mythological blacksmith figure?
choices: The Marriage of Heaven and Hell

questions.q: Which Dutch master's self-portraits demonstrate his evolving artistic style across multiple decades, with over 60 known examples?

In 'Wuthering Heights,' what is the name of the orphan boy brought into the Earnshaw household who becomes the central figure of the novel's passion and revenge?

Which modernist poet wrote 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock,' a work that captures the paralysis of indecision in urban life?
What is the primary artistic movement associated with Salvador Dalí, characterized by dreamlike imagery and irrational juxtapositions?
In 'Frankenstein,' what is the name of the creature's creator, the ambitious scientist whose experiment goes catastrophically wrong?

choices: Physical changes of characters into other forms

questions.q: Which Irish author wrote 'Ulysses,' a modernist novel that parallels Homer's 'Odyssey' and is set in Dublin?

What artistic style did René Magritte practice, characterized by incongruous and thought-provoking imagery like 'The Son of Man'?
In James Joyce's 'Ulysses', which Dublin newspaper office does Leopold Bloom visit in the 'Aeolus' episode?
